title: Prenatal exposure to organophosphate pesticides and neurobehavioral development of neonates: a birth cohort study in Shenyang, China.
ehr_evidence: None; data were obtained via questionnaires and urine analysis.
summary: A birth cohort study in Shenyang, China, investigating the association between prenatal organophosphate pesticide (OP) exposure and neonatal neurobehavioral development.
key_findings: Prenatal total DAP concentrations were inversely associated with scores on the NBNA scales.
A 10-fold increase in DAP concentrations was associated with a decrease of 1.78 regarding the Summary NBNA.
There was an estimated 2.11-point difference in summary NBNA scores between neonates in the highest quintile of prenatal OP exposure and the lowest quintile group.
captured_features: Urinary DAP metabolites (DMP, DMTP, DEP, DETP)
population: 249 pregnant women and their neonates in Shenyang, China
